etymology.description: 來(來) (lái, "come") plus 吧(吧) (ba, "particle for suggestion/softening") forms this expression which is often used to prompt someone to proceed.
etymology.interpretation: 來(來) (lái, "come") directly invites or asks someone to come forward or approach. 吧(吧) (ba, "particle for suggestion/softening") moderates the tone, turning a direct command or statement into a softer suggestion or encouragement. The combination "來吧" (lái ba) thus becomes an invitation or urging, often with an encouraging or friendly tone — as in urging someone to start or participate.

parts.english: particle for suggestion; softening particle; urging particle

examples.chinese: 我们一起去看电影，好吗(我們一起去看電影，好嗎)？来吧(來吧)，会很有趣(會很有趣)！
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yīqǐ qù kàn diànyǐng, hǎo ma? Lái ba, huì hěn yǒuqù!
examples.english: Let's go watch a movie together, okay? Come on, it will be fun!

examples.chinese: 来吧(來吧)，我们要迟到了(我們要遲到了)！
examples.pinyin: Lái ba, wǒmen yào chídào le!
examples.english: Come on, we are going to be late!

examples.chinese: 如果你想参加比赛(如果你想參加比賽)，就来吧(就來吧)！
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ xiǎng cānjiā bǐsài, jiù lái ba!
examples.english: If you want to join the competition, then come on!
